name: Haass, Richard
resolved_employer: Linebarger Goggan Blair & Sampson, LLP (Chief Operating Officer; former CTO)
evidence: Richard Haass (CISM/CISA) is COO of Linebarger Goggan Blair & Sampson, the large Texas government-debt-collection law firm; he relocated to San Antonio (zip 78232 is a north San Antonio zip, consistent). Linebarger holds delinquent-tax collection contracts with Travis County, which corroborates a San Antonio law-firm executive donating to Travis County commissioners (Travillion, Shea, Howard).

evidence: Stephan D. "Steve" Beuerlein of Austin is President/Owner of Burlington Ventures, Inc., founded 1992 to renovate and develop multifamily projects in Central Austin; also a UT President's Associates / Chancellor's Council member. name: Winn, Stephen
evidence: No zip/occupation/employer on the record. FEC profile (576 donations, lean ~0.001 = heavily Republican) is consistent with a wealthy GOP mega-donor; the best-known heavy-giving 'Stephen Winn' is the Dallas billionaire RealPage founder (multifamily rent-pricing software; UT-Austin $50M donor). But giving to a Travis County Democrat (Ann Howard) is anomalous and there is no zip/location to corroborate, so identity is unconfirmed and no industry is asserted. NOTE if later confirmed: RealPage = multifamily/pro-landlord relevance.

name: McGarr, Cappy

evidence: Cappy McGarr is President of MCM Interests, LLC in Dallas, a UT-McCombs MBA who runs a concentrated-equity investment business and is one of Dallas's most prominent Democratic fundraisers/bundlers (Obama-era); co-founder of the Kennedy Center Mark Twain Prize and Library of Congress Gershwin Prize. Name, MCM Interests, Dallas 75201, 'Finance' occupation and lean ~0.99 all match. (Married into the Strauss family, a prominent Dallas Jewish philanthropic family, but no direct AIPAC/pro-Israel org role was found.)
